name: Immigration Views by Politics
description: Views on immigration policy by political affiliation. 4 political groups × 3-4 response levels. IMS Ch. 4, 18.
studyDescription: A survey of 910 US adults recorded their views on immigration policy along with their political affiliation. Respondents were classified into four political groups and chose from several immigration policy responses. This was an observational survey.

variableDescriptions.political: The respondent's political affiliation: conservative, moderate, liberal, or other
variableDescriptions.response: The respondent's preferred immigration policy: Apply for citizenship, Guest worker, Leave the country, or Not sure

sourceDetail: OpenIntro datasets (openintro::immigration). IMS Ch. 4, 18. Original source: Public Religion Research Institute (PRRI) survey.

context.parameter: association between political affiliation and immigration views
context.nullClaim: immigration views are independent of political affiliation

inferenceContexts.parameter: there is an association between political affiliation and views on immigration
inferenceContexts.claim: views on immigration policy differ by political affiliation
